name: Piper (SS-409)
events: 3 Oct 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) arrived at Newport, Rhode Island from Portsmouth, New Hampshire.
6 Oct 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN), departed Newport, Rhode Island for Bar Harbour, Maine to conduct special torpedo firing tests.
7 Oct 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) arrived at Bar Harbour, Maine from Newport, Rhode Island for special torpedo firing tests.
10 Oct 1944 Having completed the special torpedo firing tests, USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN), departed Bar Harbour, Maine for New London, Connecticut.
11 Oct 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) arrived at New London, Connecticut from Bar Harbour, Maine.
25 Oct 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) departed New London, Connecticut for Key West, Florida.
1 Nov 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) arrived at Key West, Florida from New London, Connecticut.
17 Nov 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) departed Key West, Florida for the Panama Canal Zone.
21 Nov 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) arrived in the Panama Canal Zone from Key West, Florida.
29 Nov 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) departed the Panama Canal Zone for Pearl Harbor.
16 Dec 1944 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) arrived at Pearl Harbor.
25 Jan 1945 With her trials and initial training completed USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol south of Japan.
25 Feb 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon) sank the Japanese guard boat Hosen Maru No.3 (111 GRT) with gunfire west of Miyake Jima in position 34°06'N, 138°22'E.
30 Mar 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway.
26 Apr 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. B.F. McMahon) departed from Midway for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Sea of Okhotsk.

10 Jun 1945 At 1535 Y/10, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) joined USS Plaice (T/Cdr. R.S. Andrews, USN) and USS Piper (T/Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) which were also en-route to Pearl Harbor returning from patrol in the Kuriles area. They then proceeded in company. (1)

13 Jun 1945 Around 1000 V/13, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Plaice (T/Cdr. R.S. Andrews, USN) and USS Piper (T/Cdr. B.F. McMahon, USN) ended their third, fifth and second war patrols at Pearl Harbor.
They were escorted in by USS PC-580 (Lt. A.L. Anderson, USNR) which had joined around 0500 V/13.
19 Jul 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. E.L. Beach) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Sea of Japan.
1 Aug 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. E.L. Beach) arrived at Guam.
4 Aug 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. E.L. Beach) departed from Guam for her patrol area.
11 Aug 1945 USS Piper (Cdr. E.L. Beach) sank two small Japanese vessels with gunfire south-west of Kyushu, Japan in position 31°52'N, 129°49'E.

29 Aug 1945 Around 1700 K/29, in the Sea of Japan, well to the west of the northern end off Honshu, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) and USS Piper (T/Lt.Cdr. E.L. Beach, Jr., USN) made rendezvous. Some movies and phonographic records were exchanged. (2)

1 Sep 1945 Around 2020 K/29, in the Sea of Japan, well to the west of the northern end off Honshu, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) and USS Piper (T/Lt.Cdr. E.L. Beach, Jr., USN) made rendezvous. Both had received orders to leave patrol. They now proceeded southwards to leave patrol and make another rendezvous with (an)other submarine or submarines leaving patrol. (2)

3 Sep 1945 At 0815 K/3, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) and USS Piper (T/Lt.Cdr. E.L. Beach, Jr., USN) made rendezvous with USS Stickleback (T/Cdr. H.K. Nauman, USN) in approximate position 35°26'N, 129°53'E. Course was set to transit the Nishi Suido on the surface and proceed to Guam. (3)

9 Sep 1945 Around 0700 K/9, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Piper (T/Lt.Cdr. E.L. Beach, Jr., USN) and USS Stickleback (T/Cdr. H.K. Nauman, USN) were joined by USS Torsk (T/Cdr. B.E. Lewellen, USN).
Shortly afterwards the four submarines made rendezvous with their escort to Guam, USS Steele (Lt. F. Seymour, USNR) which already had USS Pargo (T/Cdr. D.B. Bell, USN) in company.
At submarines arrived at Guam around 1100 K/9 ending their last war patrols. All were taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Sperry (T/Capt. R.J. Ramsbotham, USN). (4)

10 Sep 1945 USS Pargo (T/Cdr. D.B. Bell, USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Piper (T/Lt.Cdr. E.L. Beach, Jr., USN), USS Stickleback (T/Cdr. H.K. Nauman, USN) and USS Torsk (T/Cdr. B.E. Lewellen, USN) departed Guam for Pearl Harbor.

19 Sep 1945 USS Pargo (T/Cdr. D.B. Bell, USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Piper (T/Lt.Cdr. E.L. Beach, Jr., USN), USS Stickleback (T/Cdr. H.K. Nauman, USN) and USS Torsk (T/Cdr. B.E. Lewellen, USN) arrived at Pearl Harbor from Guam.
